Versioned, approved deliverables. Each generation of an artifact creates a new row. Prior rows are never deleted — the version history is preserved for CPOR audit and for client-facing engagement reviews.
The team member who passed Gate 3 for this artifact version.
Unique identifier for entity instances
Flags output from Evidence Reviewer. Preserved even after approval.
ocumented justification if sales team overrides an Evidence Reviewer flag. Written by the agent when the user responds "Override FLAG N: [reason]".
The team member who triggered Pipeline 3 artifact generation.
Number of confirmed hypothesis rows included in this artifact version.
Auto: "[ArtifactType] v[N] — [ClientName] — [Date]"
Most recent approved transcript at the time this artifact was generated.
Monotonically incrementing per artifact type per opportunity.
Number of wave projects included in this artifact version.

This is the foundational evidence store. Every claim the agent generates from public research or transcript processing lives here as a row. Hypotheses are never deleted or overwritten — the audit trail must survive the entire engagement lifecycle.
The team member who approved the gate that confirmed this hypothesis.
Hypothesis Category
Question to validate this hypothesis in a client meeting. Required by the Evidence Reviewer when label is Inferred or Needs Validation.
Human-readable source description, e.g. "public research — website careers page"
Unique identifier for entity instances
False when superseded by a later row. Never delete rows — mark inactive.
Populated after Lead qualification. Null during Lead stage.
Public URL if hypothesis originates from web research
The hypothesis text — what the agent claims about the client
Points to the replacing row when cpor_isactive = false.
Populated when hypothesis is created or updated from transcript evidence.
Which wave projects this hypothesis supports or gates

Description.description: A curated record of a specific regulatory obligation, legislation, or compliance requirement applicable to a particular industry code in a particular territory/jurisdiction under a particular compliance domain.
Yes = legally mandatory; No = voluntary / best practice

Authoritative bodies, directories, and databases used as research starting points. Populates Stage 4 fallback URLs when no specific regulatory registration exists for an industry × territory × domain intersection.

Semi-colon separated domain codes this source covers. Denormalized for quick lookup. e.g. "PRIV;FIN;CYBER"
e.g. "World Health Organization"

One row per meeting submission. This is the anchor for all Pipeline 2 processing and the link between the agent's reasoning and the D365 activity timeline.
The team member who passed Gate 2 for this transcript
Raw parsed attendee string from TRANSCRIPT START header
Client-side attendees (text, not CRM records)
Count of hypotheses contradicted in this transcript
Parsed from TRANSCRIPT START header
Auto: "[ClientName] — [MeetingDate]"
Net new items discovered beyond prior hypothesis set
Partner-side attendees matched to SystemUser names
The meeting activity created when this transcript was submitted
Agent-generated structured reconciliation narrative from Pipeline 2
The team member who pasted the transcript
Unique identifier for entity instances
Full assembled transcript text
Unique identifier of the user who created the record.
Date and time when the record was created.
Unique identifier of the delegate user who created the record.
Sequence number of the import that created this record.
Unique identifier of the user who modified the record.

Unique identifier for the business unit that owns the record

Status of the Transcript
Status of the Transcript
Reason for the status of the Transcript
Reason for the status of the Transcript
For internal use only.
Time zone code that was in use when the record was created.
A card form for this entity.
A form for this entity.
Active Transcripts owned by me
The prioritized project list. Carries the scoring model dimensions, wave assignment, CPOR workload mapping, and risk posture for every recommended project in the engagement.
Governance prerequisites required before AI use cases are deployed. Required by Evidence Reviewer Check 9 for any AI use case row.
AI use cases tied to this project with readiness category.
The team member who passed Gate 2 for this project row.
CPOR workload area string, e.g. "Modern Work — M365 Copilot"
Delivery difficulty (subtracted from score). 5=enterprise-wide, core systems, governance gaps.
Predecessor project. This project cannot proceed until the referenced project is delivered.
How this project improves or protects external stakeholder outcomes. Required by Evidence Reviewer Check 7.
Project display name, e.g. "Compliance Evidence Collection Workflow"
Primary hypothesis this project addresses.
Prior wave assignment before the most recent Gate 2 update.
One sentence per scoring dimension explaining the score assigned. Required by Evidence Reviewer Check 4.
Calculated score from 9-dimension scoring model. Range: -9 to +27
Change management barriers (subtracted). 5=no sponsor, competing priorities.
Business consequence if project succeeds. 5=revenue/safety/license-to-operate.
Data availability/quality gaps (subtracted). 5=no usable data, major governance gaps.
Quality of Microsoft solution alignment. 5=confirmed fit with direct CPOR workload.
Compliance consequence of not acting. 5=confirmed obligation with near-term deadline.
Harm or benefit to external stakeholders. 5=direct harm or regulatory action risk.
Named executive priority signal. 5=executive named as top-3 priority in transcript.
Urgency of time pressure. 5=immediate (deadline within 60 days).
Incremented when wave assignment changes after approval.

Active Wave Projects owned by me
Type the name of the company associated with the lead. This becomes the account name when the lead is qualified and converted to a customer account.
Type the first name of the primary contact for the lead to make sure the prospect is addressed correctly in sales calls, email, and marketing campaigns.
Combines and shows the lead's first and last names so the full name can be displayed in views and reports.
Type of industry with which the company or organization of the lead is associated.

label.description: Agriculture and Non-petrol Natural Resource Extraction

Description.description: Select the primary industry in which the lead's business is focused, for use in marketing segmentation and demographic analysis.
Type the last name of the primary contact for the lead to make sure the prospect is addressed correctly in sales calls, email, and marketing campaigns.
Type a subject or descriptive name, such as the expected order, company name, or marketing source list, to identify the lead.
Type the website URL for the company associated with this lead.
A form for this entity.

label.description: Project Wave And AI Use Case Readiness Brief

Description.description: (from public evidence or transcript)
(consistent with public evidence)
(follows from industry/product/context)
(no public basis, requires client confirmation)
(insufficient information)
(transcript evidence explicitly contradicts)
(TRANSCRIPT START received, PhoneCall created)
(Pipeline 2 running)
(waiting for human approval)
(Gate 2 passed — immutable record)
(sales team declined)
(2-8 weeks)
(2-6 months)
(6-18+ months)
(Assessment and validation)
(Quick wins and risk reduction)
(Process modernization and data foundation)
(AI-enabled operations and advanced use cases)
(Strategic transformation and operating model change)
Shows an overview of data related to opportunities.
Team Member access to the Dynamics 365 Sales app module.
Easy-to-use sales tools, with AI-generated insights from Copilot.
